notes: s/frame measured via gRPC benchmark, may vary with concurrent GPU load
5B model needs guidance_scale >= 5.0 for prompt adherence
A14B Lightning is distilled for guidance_scale=1.0
49f spike in benchmarks was GPU contention, not real

causal_inference_guide.what_it_does: Splits video into overlapping chunks of N frames. pad=P means P frames overlap between adjacent chunks for temporal blending.
causal_inference_guide.overhead: Each pad frame is computed TWICE (once per chunk). With causal=17 pad=5, a 33f clip = 3 chunks with 10 wasted overlap frames (30% overhead).
rules: num_frames <= 33: disable causal (0/0) — whole clip fits in memory on 48GB
num_frames 33-49: causal=33 pad=3 — 2 chunks, minimal overlap
num_frames 49-81: causal=41 pad=3 — 2-3 chunks, lean overlap
pad=0 is fastest but may cause visible seams at chunk boundaries
pad=3 is minimum for smooth blending, pad=5 is conservative
causal_inference_guide.benchmark_evidence: 9f no_causal was 10% faster than causal=17/pad=5 (64.8s vs 72.2s)

a14b_lightning_fast._description: A14B Lightning 33f — designed for 4 steps, best prompt adherence at low step count
a14b_lightning_fast._causal_note: 33f fits in memory without chunking on 48GB

a14b_lightning_lowres._description: A14B Lightning at lower res — 34% faster than full res

5b_speed._description: 5B ti2v — fastest raw speed, but only 4 steps may underdeliver on prompt. TEST QUALITY.
5b_speed._warning: 4 steps with no Lightning LoRA — quality may be poor. Use 5b_balanced instead.

5b_balanced._description: 5B ti2v — good balance of speed and quality. 10 steps with proper CFG.

5b_quality._description: 5B ti2v — maximum quality at full resolution. Slower but best 5B output.

5b_81f_fast._description: 5B ti2v 81-frame clip — fastest path to a full-length video
5b_81f_fast._causal_note: 81f needs chunking. 41/3 = 2 chunks with only 3 overlap frames (vs 17/5 = 5 chunks with 20 overlap frames wasted)

a14b_lightning_81f._description: A14B Lightning 81-frame — slower but reliable prompt adherence at 4 steps
a14b_lightning_81f._benchmark: estimated ~750-900s at 640x384 based on per-frame scaling
a14b_lightning_81f._causal_note: A14B is larger than 5B — 81f definitely needs chunking. 41/3 = 2 lean chunks vs 17/5 = 5 wasteful chunks

observations: 5B model is 1.5-2x faster than A14B at same resolution/steps
33 frames is the efficiency sweet spot (3.2s/f vs 6.3s/f at 16f)
TeaCache has no effect at 4 steps (needs warmup steps)
Resolution matters less at short clips, more at 49+ frames
DDIM_TRAILING sampler is faster than UNIPC_TRAILING for 5B
guidance_scale=1.0 was used in all benchmarks — 5B needs 5.0+ for prompt adherence
A14B Lightning is trained for guidance_scale=1.0, no need to increase
Disabling causal inference for <=33f clips saves ~10% (no wasted overlap computation)
Old causal=17/pad=5 on 33f = 3 chunks with 30% overlap waste; new causal=0 = single pass
